title: Factor analysis of mixed (numeric + categorical) data
summary: FAMD in the FactoMineR tradition: standardise numerics, disjunctively code categoricals with the (I_k - p_k)/sqrt(p_k) transform, and run SVD on the concatenated matrix. Emits per-batch dimension summaries plus per-variable and per-level coordinate/contribution/cos² tables.
description: For each batch and each n_factors in [min_count, max_count]: complete-case filter, split into numeric and qualitative columns, drop any variable with zero variance or a single-level factor, standardise numerics with n-1 SD, disjunctive code categoricals as (indicator - proportion)/sqrt(proportion), horizontally concatenate, and compute thin SVD of X/sqrt(n). Dimension coordinates are σ·V, contributions are V²·100 (%), cos² is coord²/total per item. Per-variable rows aggregate per-level contributions (categorical) or take the single quantitative row directly. Sheet names follow `<batch>_k<n>_sum`, `<batch>_k<n>_vars`, `<batch>_k<n>_cats` (only emitted when categorical levels exist), truncated to Excel's 31-char limit.
usage:
[["factor-analysis::famd"]]
input = "./data.csv"
meta = "./meta.csv"
run_dir = "./runs"
run_name = "famd1"
min_count = 2
max_count = 5

arguments.name: meta
arguments.description: Variable metadata CSV. Column `type` drives encoding: numeric is standardised, dichotomous/ordinal/categorical are disjunctively coded.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run; no '..', '/' or '\\'.

arguments.name: batches
arguments.description: Subset of batches to analyse. Empty = one FAMD per batch, or a single `all` batch if meta has no batch column.

arguments.name: min_count
arguments.description: Smallest number of dimensions per batch.

arguments.name: max_count
arguments.description: Largest number of dimensions per batch.

arguments.name: na_action
arguments.description: complete_cases (listwise) or fail (abort on NA). FAMD is always listwise per batch.

arguments.name: headers
arguments.description: Whether CSV inputs have a header row.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Number of worker threads for the batch × n_factors fan-out. `1` (default) runs serially. Results are deterministic regardless of thread count.

arguments.name: output_format
arguments.description: Override stdout format: human, protocol, or quiet.

notes: Variables with zero variance (numeric) or a single level (categorical) are dropped from a batch with a warning.
`strongest_dim` / `strongest_abs_coord` identify the axis on which each variable or level loads most heavily.
